title: asplenium subtrialatum
intro: Asplenium subtrialatum, also known as the black spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Asplenium subtrialatum include reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and protecting against microbial infections. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be applied topically to affected areas as a poultice or cream.
